content.subtitle: What is a conditional sentence?
items: A conditional sentence contains the word **if** and describes a situation along with its possible result.
Every conditional has two parts: the **if-clause** (the condition) and the **main clause** (the result).
The type of conditional we choose depends on whether the situation is real, possible or imaginary.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
items: When the **if-clause** comes first, we put a comma before the result clause.
✓ **If the weather improves, we'll go hiking.**
When the result clause comes first, a comma is not usually needed.
✓ **We'll stay inside if it rains later.**

content.subtitle: Use
rows: To talk about general truths, habits or scientific facts

content.subtitle: Form
content.formula: if + present simple, will + bare infinitive

content.subtitle: Use
rows: To talk about real or likely future situations
If we **leave** now, we **'ll catch** the train.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
items: **If you practise more, you might improve quickly.**
**If you're tired, go to bed earlier!**

content.subtitle: Form
content.formula: if + past simple, would + bare infinitive

content.subtitle: Use
rows: To talk about imaginary or unlikely situations
If I **won** the lottery, I **would travel** around the world.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
items: **If I were you, I'd apologise immediately.**
**If I was you, I'd take a break.**

sections.title: A: Zero conditional
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct present simple form.

items.sentence: If you ...... (freeze) water, it ...... (become) ice.
If children ...... (not sleep) enough, they ...... (feel) tired.
If people ...... (exercise) regularly, they usually ...... (stay) healthy.
If I ...... (eat) too late, I ...... (have) stomach problems.
If we ...... (combine) red and white, we ...... (make) pink.
If students ...... (not listen), they ...... (miss) important information.

sections.title: B: First conditional
sections.instructions: Complete the first conditional sentences with the correct verb forms.

items.sentence: If the weather ...... (improve), we ...... (go) to the beach.
If you ...... (practise) every day, you ...... (improve) quickly.
If I ...... (meet) Jake later, I ...... (invite) him to dinner.
If they ...... (catch) the early train, they ...... (arrive) before noon.
If we ...... (not leave) soon, we ...... (be) late.
If you ...... (feel) sick tomorrow, ...... (stay) at home.

sections.title: C: Second conditional
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct second conditional form.

items.sentence: If I ...... (know) another language, I ...... (work) abroad.
If she ...... (have) more confidence, she ...... (speak) in public more often.
If we ...... (own) a bigger house, we ...... (invite) more guests.
If he ...... (not waste) so much time online, he ...... (finish) earlier.
If they ...... (live) closer, we ...... (see) them every weekend.
If I ...... (be) you, I ...... (accept) the offer.

sections.title: D: Choose the correct conditional
sections.instructions: Circle the correct option in each sentence.

items.sentence: If you **touch / touched** fire, it **burns / would burn**.
If she **works / worked** harder, she **will succeed / succeeds**.
If I **am / were** famous, I **would help / help** charities.
If we **don't charge / didn't charge** the battery, the phone **dies / will die**.
If they **take / took** a taxi now, they **will arrive / arrive** earlier.
If he **was / were** more organised, he **wouldn't forget / doesn't forget** deadlines.

sections.title: E: Correct the errors
sections.instructions: Each sentence has an error in a conditional structure. Write the whole sentence correctly.

items.sentence: If I would know the answer, I would tell you.
items.answer: If I knew the answer, I would tell you.

items.sentence: If he will leave now, he will catch the bus.
items.answer: If he leaves now, he will catch the bus.

items.sentence: Plants die if they won't get water.
items.answer: Plants die if they don't get water.

items.sentence: If you would practise more, you would improve quickly.
items.answer: If you practised more, you would improve quickly.

items.sentence: If I were you, I wouldn't stayed there.
items.answer: If I were you, I wouldn't stay there.

items.sentence: Unless she will hurry, she'll miss the lesson.
items.answer: Unless she hurries, she'll miss the lesson.

sections.title: F: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using a maximum of four words, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence.

items.sentence:
Metal expands when it gets hot.
...... metal, it expands.

Maybe we won't arrive before dark.
We ...... before dark.

She never practises, so she doesn't improve.
If she ......, she would improve.

He isn't fit because he never exercises.
If he exercised regularly, he ...... fitter.

I don't own a car, so I walk everywhere.
If I ...... a car, I wouldn't walk everywhere.

People become stressed when they work too much.
...... too much, people become stressed.
